original_abstract: This research explores the implementation and impact of religious character education programs on the daily lives of students at MI Nurul Yaqin Surabaya. Employing a descriptive qualitative approach, the study gathered data through interviews, field observations, and documentation. The findings indicate that the madrasah has successfully established a well-structured and functional religious character education framework, integrated through daily routines such as morning assemblies, Quranic recitation (PBA), and congregational prayers. While current programs are running smoothly, the study identifies several areas for refinement to maximize student engagement and effectiveness. Based on these results, it is recommended that the madrasah conduct systematic evaluations, provide specialized pedagogical training for teachers, and foster stronger partnerships with parents to reinforce religious values within the home environment. By adopting these strategies, MI Nurul Yaqin can ensure its character-building initiatives achieve their intended goals and contribute meaningfully to the holistic moral development of its students.
humanized_abstract: This study aims to examine the implementation and the impact of religious character education program on the students at MI Nurul Yaqin Surabaya. The study was done descriptively using qualitative research method and the data was collected from interviews, field observation and documentation. The result of this study show that that the implementation of religious character education at MI Nurul Yaqin Surabaya have made a smooth and effective establishment of educational structure of the Islamic Character Education which is consistently implemented into each academic activity held at the school such as morning assembly, PBA (Daily Muraqabah upon the Quran) and the school congregation prayer. However, it is found that there are many points of improvement needed to be improved in order to make the implementation of the educational structure more effective toward students engagement. This study then gives several recommendations such as holding an assessment to gain deeper understanding of what the teacher needs and giving pedagogical training to the teachers to make them have confidence in implementing it as well as by increasing the involvement of parents in implementing the values that have been conveyed by the school, thereby making them more applicable in the home environment.
